mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which typically translates into robust consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid decline in the Federal Funds Rate signals an aggressive monetary easing stance by the central bank, intended to stimulate economic activity. This typically lowers the cost of capital and boosts investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops > 0.5 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances following a FEDFUNDS drop of this magnitude during identified easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in retail sales and a rise in the unemployment rate indicate a severe deterioration in consumer demand and labor market health. This combination signals an impending economic contraction, negatively impacting corporate profits and equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < -0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both retail sales contraction and unemployment rise conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of established economic expansion, robust growth in industrial production reflects strong manufacturing activity and business investment. This indicates healthy corporate earnings potential, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: In periods identified as economic expansion, SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances where INDPRO shows strong growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, sudden increase in the unemployment rate signals a rapid deterioration in the labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending. This directly impacts corporate revenues and profitability, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a very short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation erodes real corporate earnings and consumer purchasing power, often prompting tighter monetary policy. This leads to higher discount rates and increased uncertainty, negatively impacting equity valuations over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL shows sustained year-over-year growth above 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining housing starts and a rising federal funds rate indicates a tightening financial environment and weakening demand in a key cyclical sector. This typically precedes broader economic slowdowns and negative equity market performance over a longer horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average < 1200 (thousands of units) AND FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where both housing starts are low and FEDFUNDS is high.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery phase, strong month-over-month growth in retail sales signifies a robust rebound in consumer confidence and spending. This directly translates into higher corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: During identified post-recession recovery periods, SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where RSAFS shows strong monthly growth.
